evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Defines the APB-visible GPIO lock register.
Defines the GPIO lock state used by the module.
Shows the software-controlled APB write condition for register updates.
Shows the GPIO lock register itself is directly overwritten by APB write data without an additional guard.

evidence_trace.excerpt: if(r_gpio_lock[0] == '0) pwdata_l = PWDATA; ... if(r_gpio_lock[2] == '0) pwdata_l = PWDATA;
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows r_gpio_lock is intended to mediate some GPIO writes, making unguarded writes to the lock state security-relevant.
Connects apb_gpio to the SoC peripheral APB bus, confirming the software-visible access path.

hit_summary: Agent should identify that REG_GPIOLOCK/r_gpio_lock is software-writable through the APB path and lacks a guard protecting the lock register itself.
notes: Confirmed from RTL. Intended security requirement comes from official Hack@DAC18 bug description.

evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Defines the APB-visible GPIO lock register.
Defines the GPIO lock state.
Shows the low-active reset branch for GPIO register state.
Shows reset clears the GPIO lock state.
Shows r_gpio_lock controls protected GPIO behavior, so clearing it on reset affects protection state.

hit_summary: Agent should identify that reset clears r_gpio_lock and explain why this breaks GPIO lock/protection state lifecycle.
notes: Confirmed from RTL. Intended persistence/security requirement comes from official Hack@DAC18 bug description.

source_entity: debug/JTAG requester entering advanced debug TAP

evidence_trace.excerpt: reg passchk; reg [31:0] correct; reg [31:0] pass; reg [4:0] bitindex;
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Defines the state used by the debug password-checking logic.

evidence_trace.excerpt: if(tms_pad_i && (passchk)) ... if(correct >= 32'h0001_FFFF) passchk = 1; else if(tdi_o == pass[bitindex]) begin correct++; bitindex++; end
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows debug access is controlled by cumulative bit-match state rather than a complete password comparison.
Shows the TAP selects the debug instruction path.
Connects the TAP-selected debug path to the advanced debug module.

hit_summary: Agent should identify incorrect debug password-checking logic in adbg_tap_top.v, supported by passchk/correct/bitindex evidence.
notes: Confirmed from RTL. This case is scored as incorrect auth logic, distinct from H18-010 partial check and H18-012 residual state.

source_entity: debug/JTAG requester entering advanced debug TAP

evidence_trace.excerpt: reg [31:0] correct; reg [31:0] pass; reg [4:0] bitindex;
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows the password is 32 bits and the implementation uses bit-indexed state.
Confirms the expected password value is 32 bits.

evidence_trace.excerpt: if(correct >= 32'h0001_FFFF) passchk = 1; else if(tdi_o == pass[bitindex]) begin correct++; bitindex++; end
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows authorization is granted by a counter threshold over bit matches rather than a complete 32-bit equality check.

hit_summary: Agent should identify incomplete/partial debug password checking and cite the threshold/counter logic in adbg_tap_top.v.
notes: Official wording says only 31 of 32 bits are checked; scoring should require incomplete password-check semantics and RTL evidence, not an unsupported exact missing-bit claim.

evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Defines the debug authorization flag.
Shows passchk is cleared in test-logic-reset.
Shows passchk is set after successful threshold and then controls later debug scan transition.

hit_summary: Agent should identify that passchk remains as residual authorization state after successful password check and is not cleared as part of successful debug access lifecycle.
notes: Confirmed from RTL. This case is scored as post-success auth-state residue, distinct from H18-009/H18-010 password-check correctness.

mediation_or_guard: missing current privilege check in zero-riscy CSR decode/write path
sink_resource: privileged machine CSRs such as mstatus/mepc/mcause

evidence_trace.excerpt: output logic csr_access_o; output logic [1:0] csr_op_o; output logic csr_status_o;
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows the decoder emits CSR access controls without a current privilege input in this interface.
CSR instruction decode checks operation encoding but does not compare CSR privilege against current privilege level.
Connects decoded CSR operation into the CSR register file write path.

evidence_trace.excerpt: 12'h300: if (csr_we_int) ... 12'h341: if (csr_we_int) mepc_n = csr_wdata_int; 12'h342: if (csr_we_int) mcause_n = ...
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows privileged machine CSR state is updated when CSR write enable is active.
Shows CSR write operations produce write behavior without privilege mediation inside this block.

hit_summary: Agent should identify missing privilege mediation for privileged CSR writes in the zero-riscy CSR decode/write path.
notes: Confirmed from RTL. Do not score evidence from the separate ips/riscv decoder as a hit for this zero-riscy case.

mediation_or_guard: missing core_secure_mode_i guard on interrupt register writes

evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows the interrupt controller has a secure-mode input and APB register interface.
Shows write eligibility is derived from APB write signals.
Shows interrupt mask state is writable from APB without secure-mode condition.
Shows interrupt pending state is writable from APB without secure-mode condition.
Shows interrupt acknowledge state is writable from APB without secure-mode condition.

hit_summary: Agent should identify that interrupt controller registers are writable through APB without requiring core_secure_mode_i.
notes: Confirmed from RTL. Core evidence is the APB write path for r_mask/r_int/r_ack and absence of core_secure_mode_i in those write conditions.

source_entity: software/APB access to UDMA/SPI master address range

evidence_trace.excerpt: Each APB master receives START/END address definitions.

hit_summary: Agent should report an APB address aliasing/range decode problem, supported by SoC/APB range definitions and independent APB select generation.
notes: Confirmed from RTL. This is scored as the broader APB aliasing case.

Confirms SPI/UDMA is a bus-visible APB peripheral.
Confirms SoC control is a bus-visible APB peripheral.

hit_summary: Agent should identify the GPIO, SPI/UDMA, and SoC-control APB address overlap and explain the resulting peripheral-isolation failure.
notes: Confirmed from RTL. H18-008 requires GPIO involvement and is distinct from H18-001.
